description: Evidence-Based Coaching — Evolve the core program from 1 session per illusion to 3 layer-differentiated sessions connected by an evidence loop

userStories.title: Schema migration for evidence-based coaching
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create the database migration that adds layer tracking columns and constraint updates, so that the application can store evidence-based coaching data.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration file exists at supabase/migrations/20260209_evidence_based_coaching.sql
user_progress table has layer_progress JSONB DEFAULT '{}' column
conversations table has observation_assignment TEXT column
check_in_schedule table has observation_assignment TEXT and cancellation_reason TEXT columns
check_in_schedule check_in_type constraint accepts 'evidence_bridge'
check_in_schedule status constraint accepts 'cancelled'
Existing users with illusions_completed get all 3 layers set in layer_progress for those illusions
In-progress users with completed core conversations for current illusion get ['intellectual'] in layer_progress for that illusion
In-progress users with NO completed core conversations for current illusion do NOT get layer_progress entry for that illusion
New users with no progress get layer_progress = '{}'
Migration does not overwrite existing layer_progress data if already present
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Run npm run db:types after migration to regenerate TypeScript types. See REQ-50 for migration logic details. Reference existing migration patterns in supabase/migrations/.

userStories.title: Create layer instruction prompt blocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want 3 layer instruction prompt blocks that define the AI's coaching approach for each layer (analytical, emotional, identity-forward), so that sessions have distinct character.
acceptanceCriteria: File server/utils/prompts/layer-instructions.ts exists
Exports LAYER_1_INTELLECTUAL_INSTRUCTIONS, LAYER_2_EMOTIONAL_INSTRUCTIONS, LAYER_3_IDENTITY_INSTRUCTIONS as string constants
L1 instructions include Socratic questioning guidance, CBT evidence examination, MI reflective listening
L1 instructions include session flow (surface belief, explore, reframe, discover contradiction, solidify)
L1 instructions include {observationTemplate} placeholder and [OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: ...] token instructions
L2 instructions include emotional holding guidance and evidence bridge opening ('What have you been noticing?')
L2 instructions include {observationTemplate} placeholder and [OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: ...] token instructions
L3 instructions include identity integration guidance, evidence + feelings bridge opening
L3 instructions include illusion completion marking ('You've seen through the [Illusion Name]')
L3 instructions include {nextIllusionPreview} placeholder for next-illusion tease
L3 instructions explicitly state NO [OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: ...] token
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference docs/guides/coaching-framework-guide.md Section 6 for session flow patterns. L1/L2 have {observationTemplate} placeholder replaced at assembly time. L3 has {nextIllusionPreview} placeholder replaced at assembly time. See the spec's Prompt Design section for exact template content.

userStories.title: Add observation templates to illusion prompt files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want observation assignment templates exported from each illusion prompt file, so that the AI has structured templates to personalize and a fallback exists if AI personalization fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 5 illusion prompt files exports an OBSERVATION_TEMPLATES map
Each OBSERVATION_TEMPLATES has 'intellectual' and 'emotional' keys (no 'identity' key)
Stress Relief intellectual template references stress timing and last use
Stress Relief emotional template references feeling the stress-nicotine connection
Pleasure intellectual template references paying attention to actual physical sensation
Pleasure emotional template references difference between wanting and enjoying
Willpower intellectual template references predicting vs actual craving outcome
Willpower emotional template references the fear around cravings
Focus intellectual template references tracking focus dips across a day
Focus emotional template references how focus dips feel
Identity intellectual template references when 'addict' label appears
Identity emotional template references feeling of questioning the label
All 10 templates are complete sentences ready to display as-is on the session-complete screen
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files are in server/utils/prompts/illusions/. 10 templates total (5 illusions x 2 layers). See the spec's Observation Templates section for exact template text. Templates match the themes in the Per-Illusion Journey Summary table.

userStories.title: Evolve prompt assembly to inject layer instructions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want buildSystemPrompt to accept illusionLayer and inject the corresponding layer instruction block after the illusion prompt, so that each session gets the correct coaching approach.
acceptanceCriteria: buildSystemPrompt accepts optional illusionLayer parameter ('intellectual' | 'emotional' | 'identity')
When illusionLayer is 'intellectual', Layer 1 instructions are injected after illusion prompt and before bridge context
When illusionLayer is 'intellectual' and illusionKey is provided, {observationTemplate} placeholder is replaced with the correct template from that illusion file's OBSERVATION_TEMPLATES
When illusionLayer is 'emotional', Layer 2 instructions with correct template replacement are injected
When illusionLayer is 'identity', Layer 3 instructions are injected with NO {observationTemplate}
When illusionLayer is 'identity' and illusionKey is NOT the final illusion, {nextIllusionPreview} is replaced with next-illusion preview text
When illusionLayer is 'identity' and illusionKey is 'identity' (final illusion), {nextIllusionPreview} is replaced with ceremony tease
When illusionLayer is omitted, no layer instructions are injected (backwards compatible)
Prompt assembly order: base → personalization → cross-layer context → illusion prompt → layer instructions → bridge context → abandoned session → opening
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/utils/prompts/index.ts. Add illusionLayer to BuildSystemPromptOptions. Import layer instructions and observation templates. Insert after illusion prompt per REQ-36. The {nextIllusionPreview} in L3 is replaced based on illusion order position.

userStories.title: Extract observation assignment from chat streaming response
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the chat streaming handler to extract [OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: ...] from the AI's response after [SESSION_COMPLETE] and store it on the conversations table.
acceptanceCriteria: After [SESSION_COMPLETE] is detected in the full response, the handler parses for [OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: ...] token
When token is found (e.g., '[OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT: Notice your stress patterns]'), the text 'Notice your stress patterns' is stored on conversations.observation_assignment
When token is NOT found after SESSION_COMPLETE, conversations.observation_assignment is set to null
When SESSION_COMPLETE is not in the response, no observation extraction is attempted
Multi-line observation text is handled correctly (whitespace trimmed, content preserved)
Extraction works in both streaming and non-streaming onComplete paths
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/chat.post.ts. Regex: /\[OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT:\s*([\s\S]*?)\]/. Store alongside existing session_completed=true update. Look for existing [SESSION_COMPLETE] detection pattern and add observation extraction after it.

userStories.title: Evolve useProgress composable for layer data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the useProgress composable to expose layer-specific computed properties (currentLayer, layerSessionNumber, layersCompletedForIllusion), so that components can display layer progress.
acceptanceCriteria: useProgress exports currentLayer computed that derives next incomplete layer from layer_progress for current illusion
LAYER_ORDER constant is ['intellectual', 'emotional', 'identity']
currentLayer returns 'intellectual' when layer_progress is empty or null
useProgress exports layersCompletedForIllusion(illusionKey) function returning string[]
layersCompletedForIllusion returns empty array for unknown illusion key
useProgress exports layerSessionNumber computed (completed count + 1)
useProgress exports layerProgress computed returning the full layer_progress object
completeSession method accepts optional illusionLayer parameter and passes it to the API
When progress is null (not loaded), all computed properties return safe defaults
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: composables/useProgress.ts. Current layer is derived (next incomplete in ordered list ['intellectual', 'emotional', 'identity']), not stored. See REQ-29. Uses getCurrentIllusionKey() helper for deriving the illusion key from illusion_order + current_illusion.

userStories.title: Evolve complete-session API for layer progression (L1/L2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want complete-session.post.ts to handle layer progression for L1 and L2, advancing the user to the next layer within the same illusion instead of completing the illusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Request body accepts optional illusionLayer field ('intellectual' | 'emotional' | 'identity')
When illusionLayer is 'intellectual', layer_progress is updated to include 'intellectual' for that illusion
When illusionLayer is 'emotional', layer_progress is updated to include 'emotional' for that illusion
Response includes layerCompleted, nextLayer, isIllusionComplete=false, and observationAssignment fields for L1/L2
observationAssignment uses conversation's extracted text when available
observationAssignment falls back to OBSERVATION_TEMPLATES template text when conversation's observation_assignment is null
When illusionLayer doesn't match the derived current layer from layer_progress, returns 409 Conflict with correct layer
illusionLayer is validated against allowed values; invalid values return 400
When illusionLayer is omitted (legacy client), behaves as before (marks entire illusion complete) for backwards compatibility
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/progress/complete-session.post.ts. Derives current layer from layer_progress (next incomplete in ordered list). Validates against request. Uses jsonb_set or app-level merge for layer_progress JSONB. Read observation_assignment from conversations table for the conversationId.

userStories.title: Evolve complete-session API for layer progression (L3 — illusion complete)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want complete-session.post.ts to handle L3 completion by marking the illusion as complete and auto-unlocking the next illusion.
acceptanceCriteria: When illusionLayer is 'identity', layer_progress includes all 3 layers and the illusion is added to illusions_completed
Response includes isIllusionComplete=true, nextLayer=null, observationAssignment=null
nextIllusion is calculated using existing logic and returned in response
For the final illusion (identity), isComplete=true and nextIllusion=null are returned
Conviction assessment records the illusion_layer value ('identity') for L3 completions
Any pending evidence_bridge check-ins for this user/illusion are cancelled with reason 'illusion_completed'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/progress/complete-session.post.ts. This is the L3 branch of the layer progression logic added in US-007. When identity illusion L3 completes, set program_status='ceremony_ready' and ceremony_ready_at=now() (existing behavior from ceremony spec).

userStories.title: Pass current layer to chat API from session page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the session page to determine the current layer from progress data on mount and pass it to all chat API calls, so that the AI receives correct layer instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Session page fetches progress on mount and derives current layer from layer_progress
illusionLayer is stored in a ref and passed in all chat API request bodies (opening message and user messages)
VoiceSessionView component accepts illusionLayer as a prop
useVoiceSession composable receives and passes illusionLayer to chat API request body
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: pages/session/[illusion].vue. Also update components/voice/SessionView.vue (VoiceSessionView) and composables/useVoiceSession.ts to thread illusionLayer through. The session page already has illusionLayer tracking from ceremony spec (US-013) — extend it to use progress-derived layer instead of conversation-fetched layer.

userStories.title: Configure SessionCompleteCard for layer context (L1/L2 with observation)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SessionCompleteCard to show the observation assignment and 'Continue to Next Session' CTA for L1/L2 completions.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionCompleteCard accepts showContinue boolean prop
SessionCompleteCard emits 'continue-layer' event (new, distinct from existing 'continue' emit)
When showContinue=true, 'Continue to Next Session' CTA is visible as secondary (ghost border) style
When showContinue=false, only 'Return to Dashboard' CTA is visible
For L1/L2, session page sets subtext to the observation assignment text from complete-session response
When observation assignment is null (extraction failed), subtext falls back to generic settling message with spacing recommendation
When user taps 'Continue to Next Session', session page re-initializes for the next layer (fetches updated progress, starts new session)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files: components/SessionCompleteCard.vue, pages/session/[illusion].vue. Session page uses isIllusionComplete and observationAssignment from complete-session response to configure the card. Generic fallback: 'Great work. Take a moment to let this settle. Your next session will be ready tomorrow.'

userStories.title: Configure SessionCompleteCard for L3 (no observation, illusion complete)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SessionCompleteCard to show a generic settling message with only 'Return to Dashboard' for L3 completions, and ceremony-specific copy for the final illusion.
acceptanceCriteria: For L3 completion, subtext is 'Great work. Take a moment to let this settle.' with no observation assignment
For L3 completion, showContinue is false — no 'Continue to Next Session' CTA
For L3 of the final illusion (Identity), heading is 'Session Complete' and subtext is 'All five illusions dismantled. Your final ceremony is ready.' per ceremony-spec.md
Only 'Return to Dashboard' CTA is shown for all L3 completions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: pages/session/[illusion].vue. L3 uses isIllusionComplete=true from response. Final illusion uses isComplete=true. The ceremonyTease prop on SessionCompleteCard already exists from ceremony spec US-013 — reuse it for the final illusion case.

userStories.title: Schedule evidence bridge check-ins on layer completion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the session-complete handler to schedule evidence bridge check-ins with 24-hour timing and observation-specific prompts for L1/L2 completions.
acceptanceCriteria: After L1 session completes, an evidence_bridge check-in is scheduled for ~24 hours later
After L2 session completes, an evidence_bridge check-in is scheduled for ~24 hours later
After L3 session completes, NO evidence bridge check-in is scheduled
check_in_schedule.observation_assignment is populated with the template text
check_in_schedule.prompt_template wraps the observation: 'You were going to [observation] — what did you observe?'
check_in_schedule.check_in_type is 'evidence_bridge'
When observation text is null (extraction failed), template fallback text is used for both observation_assignment and prompt_template
If check-in scheduling insert fails, session completion still succeeds (non-blocking) and error is logged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/utils/scheduling/check-in-scheduler.ts (or wherever check-ins are scheduled in the session-complete flow). Evidence bridge uses 24hr timing (REQ-28), not the 2hr default. Existing post_session check-in scheduling continues unchanged for non-layer sessions.

userStories.title: Cancel evidence bridge check-ins on next session start
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pending evidence bridge check-ins to be cancelled when the user starts the next layer session, so that redundant emails aren't sent.
acceptanceCriteria: When a new core session is created for an illusion, any scheduled or sent evidence_bridge check-ins for that user/illusion are cancelled
Cancelled check-ins have status='cancelled' and cancellation_reason='user_continued_immediately'
Reinforcement sessions do NOT trigger check-in cancellation
Cancelled check-ins are NOT re-scheduled even if the user abandons the next layer
Cancellation is non-blocking (fire-and-forget, errors logged)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/chat.post.ts — after creating a new conversation. Cancel query: update check_in_schedule where user_id, trigger_illusion_key, check_in_type='evidence_bridge', status IN ('scheduled', 'sent'). Only cancel for core sessions, not reinforcement.

userStories.title: Update check-in email and cron for evidence bridge type
userStories.description: As a developer, I want evidence bridge check-in emails to use the observation-specific prompt and be included in the cron job's query.
acceptanceCriteria: The check-in cron job's query includes 'evidence_bridge' type check-ins in its results
Evidence bridge check-in emails use the prompt_template field for email body content
The observation_assignment text appears in the email body
Evidence bridge check-in conversations use the prompt as the AI's natural opening
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing email template and check-in flow already use prompt_template. This may require minimal or no code changes — mainly verification that the cron query includes evidence_bridge type. Check server/api/cron/check-ins.get.ts and server/utils/email/check-in-sender.ts.

userStories.title: Add layer progress dots to ProgressCarousel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ProgressCarousel to show 'Session X of 3' with layer progress dots for the current illusion in the action section.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgressCarousel accepts layerProgress prop (Record<string, string[]>)
When current illusion is focused with 0 layers completed, shows 'Session 1 of 3' with 3 empty dots
When current illusion is focused with 1 layer completed, shows 'Session 2 of 3' with 1 filled + 2 empty dots
When current illusion is focused with 2 layers completed, shows 'Session 3 of 3' with 2 filled + 1 empty dot
Filled dots have full opacity, empty dots have 0.35 opacity
Filled dots are slightly larger (9px) than empty dots (8px) for color vision support
Layer progress dots have aria-label describing state (e.g., '1 of 3 sessions complete')
Completed or locked illusions do NOT show layer progress dots
Progress line uses text-sm text-white-65 styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: components/dashboard/ProgressCarousel.vue. Layer progress line goes in action section below illusion title, above description. Current layer is derived from layerProgress data internally (not passed separately).

userStories.title: Pass layer data from dashboard to ProgressCarousel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the dashboard page to pass layer progress data to the ProgressCarousel component.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard imports layerProgress from useProgress composable
Dashboard passes layerProgress as prop to ProgressCarousel
After session completion and dashboard navigation, carousel reflects updated layer progress
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: pages/dashboard.vue. Simple prop passing from composable to component.

userStories.title: Surface observation evidence in session context
userStories.description: As a developer, I want check-in observation responses (captured as real_world_observation moments) to appear in the AI's session context, and the moment detection pipeline to classify evidence bridge responses correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: real_world_observation moments appear in the KEY MOMENTS section of personalization context
Most recent observations for the current illusion are included within the existing 5-8 moment budget
Context assembly works correctly when no observation moments exist
Evidence bridge check-in responses create captured_moments with moment_type='real_world_observation'
Moment detection prompt/task definition includes real_world_observation as a recognized moment type with classification guidance
Evidence bridge responses like 'I noticed my stress was from the situation, not nicotine' are classified as real_world_observation (not generic insight or reflection)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: server/utils/personalization/context-builder.ts, server/utils/llm/tasks/moment-detection.ts. Verify existing context builder includes real_world_observation in its moment selection. Update moment detection prompt to recognize the new type with classification guidance for evidence bridge responses.

userStories.title: E2E test: full 3-layer session flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests covering the complete layer progression flow from L1 through L3 of a single illusion, including observation assignments, continue-immediately flow, and dashboard updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Test Flow 1: L1 completes, session-complete screen shows observation text and Continue CTA, dashboard shows 'Session 2 of 3'
Test Flow 2: User taps Continue after L1, next session uses illusionLayer='emotional', evidence bridge check-in is cancelled
Test Flow 3: L3 completes, session-complete shows generic settling text, no Continue CTA, dashboard shows illusion complete and next unlocked
Test Flow 4: Dashboard shows correct layer progress dots with opacity and aria-label
Test Flow 5: Stale client sending wrong illusionLayer to complete-session gets 409 and refreshes
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: tests/e2e/evidence-based-coaching.spec.ts. Use existing mock utilities (mockUserInProgress, mockProgressAPI) from tests/e2e/utils/. Mock chat API responses with SESSION_COMPLETE and OBSERVATION_ASSIGNMENT tokens.
